comment_text.sn10.10:1.2: As a donor once remarked, “It’s difficult for women to get material support” (Bu Pd 1:1.23). Passages like this serve to elevate nuns as worthy recipients of donations.

root_text.sn10.10:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn10.10:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro upāsako sukkāya bhikkhuniyā bhojanaṁ adāsi.
root_text.sn10.10:1.3: Atha kho sukkāya bhikkhuniyā abhippasanno yakkho rājagahe rathikāya rathikaṁ siṅghāṭakena siṅghāṭakaṁ upasaṅkamitvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:

translation_text.sn10.10:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn10.10:1.2: Now at that time a certain lay follower gave food to the nun Sukkā.
translation_text.sn10.10:1.3: Then a native spirit was so devoted to Sukkā that he went from street to street and from square to square, and on that occasion recited these verses:
translation_text.sn10.10:2.1: “O! He has made so much merit!
translation_text.sn10.10:2.2: That lay follower is so very wise.
translation_text.sn10.10:2.3: He just gave food to Sukkā,
translation_text.sn10.10:2.4: who is released from all ties.”
